Home → Discography → Thug World Order

Thug World Order

2002 · Ruthless Records

The fifth studio album by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony and their final release for Ruthless Records, not counting the later compilation T.H.U.G.S. Features the singles “Money Money“, “Get Up & Get It“ and “Home“ featuring Phil Collins. The latter charted in the UK, earning him the honorary moniker “Chrome Bone“. Flesh-n-Bone was absent due to incarceration. Bizzy Bone was ejected from the group shortly after the album's release.

"I'mma be a soldier, Runnin' from gun shots for the day I see Jehovah."

— If I Fall
01 T.W.O. Intro produced by DJ Uneek & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony feat. LaReece ▷
02 Bone, Bone, Bone produced by DJ Uneek & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
03 Guess Who's Back produced by Rico Wade feat. LaReece ▷
04 Home produced by Krayzie Bone & DJ Uneek ▷
05 What About Us? produced by Armando Colon ▷
06 Get Up & Get It produced by The Platinium Brothers feat. 3LW and Felecia ▷
07 Bad Weed Blues produced by Bosko ▷
08 All the Way produced by DJ Uneek & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
09 Non-Fiction Words by Eazy-E (Skit) produced by DJ Uneek ▷
10 Pump, Pump produced by Alvin "Stone" Clarke & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
11 Set It Straight produced by Def Jef feat. Felecia ▷
12 Money, Money produced by Self & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
13 Not My Baby produced by DJ Uneek & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
14 Cleveland Is the City produced by LT Hutton feat. Avant ▷
15 If I Fall produced by DJ Uneek & Bone Thugs-N-Harmony ▷
16 A Thug Soldier Conversation (Skit) produced by DJ Uneek ▷
